summary: Well-structured scenario set that resolves the agentish-graph concept layer into concrete acceptance flows. The given/when/then chains are causally clear and locally recoverable. Each scenario covers a distinct interaction path (open, inspect, edit, connect, move, external change, conflict resolution) with explicit success criteria, preservation guarantees, and conflict surfacing. The file correctly operates one layer below the concept file without duplicating its truths. Minor weaknesses: some entity re-declarations lack explicit traceability back to the concept layer, MoveNodeScenario is comparatively thin, and the concept file's Truth invariants are enacted implicitly rather than referenced.
strengths: Consistent fluent given/when/then structure produces high causal clarity and near-zero interpretive slack across all seven scenarios
ExternalEditor actor and ExternalChangeScenario add legitimate scope the concept layer left unresolved, demonstrating proper implementation-layer contribution
The .succeeds(), .preserves(), .conflictsAs() pattern systematically separates happy path from invariant preservation from failure surfacing — each scenario addresses all three where relevant
ResolveConflictScenario fully resolves the concept layer's SurfacedConflicts truth into an actionable human-choice flow with explicit options
High local recoverability — a reader can understand every scenario without consulting the concept file
weaknesses: Entity groups (Source, Editing, Graph) partially mirror concept-layer definitions without explicit cross-reference, creating mild traceability gap between layers
MoveNodeScenario is noticeably thinner than peers — it lacks validation, conflict handling, or edge cases around layout persistence boundaries
The concept file's named Truth invariants (SourceAuthority, DerivedProjection, etc.) are enacted by scenarios but never explicitly referenced, making it harder to verify full truth coverage
No scenario covers multi-document workspace behavior despite the concept layer declaring WorkspaceMaySpanManyDocuments as a core truth
